description: To support multi-component research resource projects and centers that will enhance the capability of resources to serve biomedical research.

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Write a concise description of this component project. Clearly state the specific aims that drive this component and define its boundaries. Crucially, explain the mechanism by which this component integrates with and supports the aims of the other components and the overall multi-project grant. Identify the specific Project Director/Principal Investigator (PD/PI) responsible for the execution and management of this component. The tone must be professional and emphasize collaborative synergy and clear management structure. Avoid generic descriptions; focus on measurable contributions to the overarching scientific goal.
guidelines: Describe the specific aims and scope of this component project within the multi-project structure.
Explain how this component integrates and contributes to the overall goals of the multi-project application.
Identify the Project Director/Principal Investigator (PD/PI) responsible for leading this specific component.
search_queries: NIH multi-project component description example
Integrating component project aims NIH grant
Role of PD/PI in multi-project component
Synergy between grant components NIH M series
Defining scope of component project R01
grant_sections.title: Overall Component Description

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Draft a highly concise, self-contained summary (abstract) of the entire project. This summary must be understandable to a scientifically literate reader outside the specific field. Include the project's central hypothesis, specific goals, the core methodology to be employed, and the expected scientific outcomes. Ensure the language is clear, compelling, and suitable for public release via NIH RePORTER if funded. Do not exceed the specified character/page limit (typically 30 lines or 4,000 characters).
guidelines: Must be self-contained and understandable to a scientifically literate reader outside the specific field.
Include the project's goals, methodology, and expected outcomes.
Ensure the summary is suitable for public release via NIH RePORTER if the application is funded.
search_queries: NIH Project Summary format RePORTER
Example NIH abstract goals methodology outcomes
Formatting project summary for NIH submission
NIH abstract character limit guidelines
grant_sections.title: Project Summary/Abstract

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Write a statement of public health relevance strictly limited to three sentences. This statement must clearly articulate how the proposed research, if successful, will improve human health, reduce disease burden, or enhance the understanding of fundamental biological processes relevant to disease. Avoid restating the scientific abstract; focus solely on the broader implications for public health and clinical practice.
guidelines: Explain the relevance of the research to public health in three sentences or less.
Describe the impact of the proposed research on improving health or understanding disease.
Avoid restating the Project Summary/Abstract; focus specifically on public health implications.
search_queries: NIH Public Health Relevance statement examples
How to write NIH relevance statement three sentences
Translational impact of basic science NIH
Public health relevance vs project summary
grant_sections.title: Public Health Relevance Statement

grant_sections.definition: Corresponds to the PHS 398 Research Plan (M.400), the core scientific narrative of the application.
grant_sections.generation_instructions: This section serves as the overarching container for the scientific proposal (Specific Aims, Research Strategy, etc.). Ensure strict adherence to NIH formatting guidelines (font, margins, line spacing) and the page limits specified in the FOA. The narrative must be logically organized, presenting a compelling case for scientific merit, feasibility, and impact. Emphasize the integration of all sub-sections (A4a, A4b) into a coherent, high-impact research program. Reviewers must be able to easily navigate the hypothesis, aims, methods, and expected results.
guidelines: Ensure all required sub-sections (Specific Aims, Research Strategy) are included and logically organized.
Adhere strictly to the page limits specified in the Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA).
Follow standard NIH formatting requirements regarding font size, margins, and line spacing.
search_queries: NIH PHS 398 Research Plan structure M.400
NIH Research Strategy page limits R01
Formatting requirements NIH grant application H series
Checklist for NIH Research Plan submission
Integrating Specific Aims and Research Strategy NIH
NIH Research Plan section organization
Scientific merit criteria NIH review
grant_sections.title: PHS 398 Research Plan
topics: Integration of Specific Aims and Strategy

grant_sections.generation_instructions: State the central hypothesis and list 2-4 highly specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) aims. Each aim should represent a distinct, logical step toward testing the hypothesis. Clearly articulate the expected outcomes for each aim and the overall impact of achieving these aims. The section must be concise, typically limited to one page, and serve as the roadmap for the entire Research Strategy. Avoid vague language or overly ambitious goals that cannot be completed within the project period.
guidelines: State concisely the goals of the proposed research.
List the specific objectives to be accomplished during the project period.
Clearly articulate the expected outcomes and the impact of achieving these aims.
search_queries: Example NIH Specific Aims section R01
Writing SMART specific aims NIH grant
Central hypothesis formulation for NIH proposal
Page limit for NIH Specific Aims
grant_sections.title: Specific Aims

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Structure this section into three mandatory sub-sections: Significance, Innovation, and Approach. Significance must justify the importance of the problem and how the project will advance scientific knowledge. Innovation must describe how the project challenges existing paradigms or uses novel concepts/methods. Approach must detail the experimental design, methodology, data analysis plan, and include a discussion of potential problems, anticipated results, and alternative strategies (risk mitigation). Preliminary data must be integrated here to demonstrate feasibility. Emphasize scientific rigor and reproducibility throughout the Approach section.
guidelines: Address Significance: Explain the importance of the problem and how the project will improve scientific knowledge.
Address Innovation: Describe how the project challenges existing paradigms or uses novel concepts/methods.
Address Approach: Detail the methodology, data analysis plan, and discuss potential problems and alternative strategies.
search_queries: NIH Research Strategy Significance Innovation Approach guidelines
Preliminary data integration NIH grant application
Discussing potential problems and alternative strategies NIH
Scientific rigor and reproducibility NIH approach section
Detailed methodology and data analysis plan R01
NIH criteria for innovation in research
Integrating preliminary data into NIH Research Strategy
grant_sections.title: Research Strategy (Significance, Innovation, Approach)

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Provide detailed justification for every line item in the budget. For personnel, clearly state the role, responsibilities, and calculate the effort in person months (e.g., 2.4 calendar months). Justify all non-personnel costs, including specific supplies, travel related directly to the project, and publication costs. If major equipment (exceeding $5,000) is requested, provide a strong rationale for its necessity and lack of availability. The justification must demonstrate cost realism and necessity for the proposed research.
guidelines: Justify all personnel costs based on effort (person months) and role in the project.
Provide detailed justification for all non-personnel costs, including supplies, travel, and publication fees.
Explain any major equipment purchases exceeding $5,000.
search_queries: NIH budget justification personnel effort calculation
Justifying major equipment purchase NIH grant
Example detailed budget justification NIH R01
Non-personnel cost justification guidelines NIH
grant_sections.title: Budget Justification

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Describe the facilities (laboratory, clinical, office, animal care) available for the project. Explain how the institutional environment and scientific setting contribute to the probability of success of the proposed research. Detail access to shared resources, core facilities, and specialized services that will be utilized, ensuring that the environment is conducive to high-quality research. If this is a multi-project grant, emphasize the collaborative environment.
guidelines: Describe the facilities available for the project (e.g., laboratory, clinical, office space).
Explain how the scientific environment contributes to the probability of success.
Detail any special equipment or resources available that are not listed in the Equipment section.
search_queries: NIH Facilities and Other Resources description example
Describing scientific environment NIH grant
Institutional support statement NIH application
Access to core facilities NIH grant writing
grant_sections.title: Facilities and Other Resources

grant_sections.generation_instructions: List all major items of equipment (typically defined as non-expendable property having a useful life of more than one year and an acquisition cost of $5,000 or more) that are already available for use in the proposed research. For each item, specify its current location and briefly describe its pertinent capabilities relevant to the project aims. If equipment is shared, state the access mechanism. Ensure consistency with the Facilities section (A6).
guidelines: List major items of equipment already available for the project.
Specify the location and pertinent capabilities of each piece of equipment.
If requesting new equipment, ensure it is justified in the Budget Justification.
search_queries: NIH Equipment section guidelines
Listing existing major instrumentation NIH grant
Example equipment list for research proposal
Defining major equipment NIH
grant_sections.title: Equipment

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Develop a detailed Data Management and Sharing (DMS) Plan compliant with the 2023 NIH Policy. Describe the scientific data to be generated, preserved, and shared. Specify the standards (e.g., metadata, format) that will be applied. Identify the chosen repository (e.g., Figshare, NCBI databases) and outline the timeline for data sharing. Detail the methods of access and any necessary restrictions to protect privacy (if applicable). Address how compliance will be monitored.
guidelines: Describe the scientific data to be managed, preserved, and shared.
Detail the standards to be applied to the scientific data and metadata.
Outline the timeline for data sharing and the methods of access.
search_queries: NIH Data Management and Sharing Plan template 2023
Selecting data repository for NIH grant
Metadata standards for scientific data sharing NIH
Budgeting for data sharing costs NIH
grant_sections.title: Data Management and Sharing (DMS) Plan

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Describe the plan for sharing research resources, including unique reagents, cell lines, animal models, and protocols developed during the project. If applicable, include a specific Genomic Data Sharing (GDS) plan detailing submission to NIH-designated repositories (e.g., dbGaP). Specify the mechanism for distribution (e.g., MTA, institutional core). If no resources will be shared, provide a clear justification for why sharing is not possible or relevant to the project.
guidelines: Describe plans for sharing research materials (e.g., model organisms, cell lines, reagents).
If applicable, include a Genomic Data Sharing (GDS) plan.
State why sharing is not possible if no resources will be shared.
search_queries: NIH Research Materials Sharing Plan guidelines
Genomic Data Sharing (GDS) plan requirements NIH
Sharing model organisms NIH grant application
Material Transfer Agreement (MTA) process NIH
grant_sections.title: Resource Sharing Plan(s)
PHS Human Subjects and Clinical Trials Information

grant_sections.generation_instructions: Provide a detailed description of all proposed procedures involving vertebrate animals. Justify the choice of species, strain, age, sex, and the exact number of animals required scientifically. Detail procedures for minimizing pain and distress, including the use of anesthetics and analgesics. Describe the method of euthanasia, ensuring consistency with the current AVMA Guidelines for the Euthanasia of Animals. Confirm institutional compliance and IACUC approval status.
guidelines: Provide a detailed description of the proposed procedures involving vertebrate animals.
Justify the species, strain, age, sex, and number of animals used.
Describe the methods of euthanasia and ensure consistency with AVMA Guidelines.
search_queries: NIH Vertebrate Animals Section requirements
Justification of animal numbers NIH grant
AVMA Guidelines for the Euthanasia of Animals
Minimizing pain and distress in animal research NIH
grant_sections.title: Vertebrate Animals

grant_sections.definition: Corresponds to the PHS 398 Career Development Award Supplemental Form (M.410).
grant_sections.generation_instructions: This section, specific to Career Development Awards, must detail the candidate's qualifications, long-term career goals, and commitment to the research area. The core focus is the structured research training plan, which must be integrated with the proposed research project. Include a strong statement from the mentor outlining their specific role, frequency of interaction, and commitment to the candidate's development into an independent investigator. Emphasize the institutional environment's support for the candidate's career trajectory.
guidelines: Describe the candidate's background, career goals, and commitment to the research area.
Detail the research training plan and the proposed research project.
Include a statement from the mentor outlining their role and commitment to the candidate's development.
search_queries: NIH K Award Candidate Background and Goals example
Writing a research training plan for K award
Mentor statement requirements NIH K grant M.410
Career development milestones NIH K application
grant_sections.title: Career Development Award Supplemental Narrative

grant_sections.definition: Corresponds to the PHS 398 Research Training Program Plan (M.420).
grant_sections.generation_instructions: Describe the overall objectives of the training program, emphasizing the institutional environment and the expertise of the training faculty. Detail the proposed training curriculum, clearly separating didactic coursework from hands-on research components. Outline the rigorous process for trainee selection, evaluation, and tracking of outcomes post-program completion. The narrative must demonstrate a commitment to diversity and the successful preparation of trainees for independent research careers.
guidelines: Describe the program's objectives, institutional environment, and training faculty.
Detail the proposed training curriculum, including didactic and research components.
Provide information on trainee selection, evaluation, and outcomes.
search_queries: NIH Research Training Program Plan M.420 example
Designing didactic and research components for T32
Trainee selection and evaluation process NIH
Program outcomes assessment for NIH training grant
grant_sections.title: Research Training Program Plan

name: Resource-Related Research Multi-Component Projects and Centers
